interpretation: Overall availability score changed from 0.55 to 1.0: IMPROVED.
Official Public RPC average response changed from 15569.0 ms to 7120.0 ms: IMPROVED.
Explorer Web average response changed from 684.0 ms to 468.0 ms: IMPROVED.
Primary Explorer API average response changed from 596.0 ms to 406.0 ms: IMPROVED.
The later observation window shows stronger overall infrastructure availability.

interpretation_guide: Window A represents the earlier observation segment.
Window B represents the later observation segment.
For availability score, higher is better.
For response time, lower is better.
Older snapshots may show N/A or UNKNOWN response classes because response-time classification was added after the initial watcher release.
This comparison report is independent observation material and not an official DAC service status page.

status_glossary.label: HEALTHY
status_glossary.meaning: The endpoint or overall infrastructure state is reachable and behaving as expected.

status_glossary.label: DEGRADED
status_glossary.meaning: The endpoint is reachable, but one or more checks or response-time indicators show reduced quality.

status_glossary.label: PARTIAL_OUTAGE
status_glossary.meaning: At least one monitored endpoint is unavailable or failing while other endpoints remain reachable.

status_glossary.label: UNHEALTHY
status_glossary.meaning: The endpoint failed required checks or did not provide usable responses.

status_glossary.label: FAST
status_glossary.meaning: The observed response-time class is fast for this watcher context.

status_glossary.label: MODERATE
status_glossary.meaning: The observed response-time class is acceptable but not fast.

status_glossary.label: SLOW
status_glossary.meaning: The observed response-time class is slow and may indicate degraded user experience.

status_glossary.label: UNKNOWN
status_glossary.meaning: The watcher could not classify the response-time state, often because older snapshots did not include this field.

status_glossary.label: N/A
status_glossary.meaning: Not available or not applicable for the selected observation, endpoint, or historical snapshot.
